channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Flag bombing in Woodmancote, 10th March
item.description:
Flags ( 25 +) have been attached to Highways infrastructure ( lamposts etc). Locations include: Two Hedges Road, Britannia Way, Cowslip Meadow, and Station Road.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 21, Whitehouse Way, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pot hole is adjacent to ironworks with a danger that will be compromised, 10th March
item.description:
Near number 27 What3words = yarn blunt visit

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 27, Keynsham Road, Fairview, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Streetlighting on Christ Church Road, 10th March
item.description:
Please consider the street-lighting on Christ Church Road, particularly between Queens Rd and Eldorado Rd. The lights do not shine on the pavement which is uneven and do not light lurking places near bushes etc. Should I walk down the road (clearly undesirable)? I do support the attempt to reduce light pollution, but this lighting is not effective.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 36, Queen's Road, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stretch of road is atrocious, 10th March
item.description:
Multiple deep pots hole that people are swerving to avoid. There's going to be an accident if not due to swerving, then motorcyclist or cyclist hitting one and coming off.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Swindon Lane, Marle Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sinking pothole which is hard to avoid several times been filled in, 10th March
item.description:
Opposite the quadrangle building as you turn left towards the ladies college on the left hand side of the carriageway past the traffic lights as you filter around the corner
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 101, Promenade, Montpellier, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 10th March
item.description:
Permanent repair Road closure day carriage pothole 800x300x70mm (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 10th March
item.description:
NWD GIVE AND TAKE 28 day PLUS 15 MINUTES DELAY BOARDS carriage pothole 800x300x70mm (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: TMC - Defects -CW6 Potholes problem, 10th March
item.description:
2 way stop and go day carriage pothole 1200x600x50mm stats required number 1 size approx (CW6 Pothole)
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grass verges, 10th March
item.description:
Grass verges along the main PE Way where residents vehicles park, damage to the ground. When Ubico staff go to mow the grass, the ground is very uneven with deep sunken rutts to the verges. This could cause an accident to the crews, causing twisted ankles, broken feet. It's a safety issue, especially using heavy machinery. Could this be addressed please.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 2, Princess Elizabeth Way, Kingsditch, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: A pothole in road problem, 10th March
item.description:
PERM - Please can a inquiry ref be raised for a Perm Repair. Details are as follows: Previous Job Number: 43118366 Address: A40 From Andoversford bypass To A436. Andoversford. USRN: 9402774 Job Description: Pothole made safe with temp repair – Requires perm repair.
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road rubble near 43 Parton Rd, 10th March
item.description:
Rubble from the potholes (which have now been filled) around 43 Parton Rd is very dangerous to cyclists and motorcyclists. Please remove.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 40, Parton Road, Churchdown, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Traffic lights stuck on red both sides, 10th March
item.description:
Kidnappers lane, Cheltenham. This morning at 7:35 the lights by the Miller Homes development were stuck on red in both directions.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kidnappers Lane, Warden Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Light sensor is not detecting vehicles

item.title: Drainage issue caused by sunken pavement, 9th March
item.description:
At the end of Cheriton Close the pavement has sunk resulting in pooled water following rain which has nowhere to drain. This makes accessing the footpath difficult, especially for elderly and small children

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 10, Cheriton Close, Hatherley, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Image from 3 yrs ago on street view but now member of public mentions broken branch that from the description is bledding heavily, considering time of year and a birch tree. Also has been overhanging path over the years becoming an obstruction as branches very low, people walking into road., 9th March
item.description:
What three words: ///others.remain.games Several years overgrown.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 44, Park Street, Marle Hill, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ongoing Neglect of Elmfield Avenue and Elmfield Street, 9th March
item.description:
I’ve been reporting the same issue for two years now, and it’s clear that giving more time won’t change anything. As shown in the attached photos, the street has been in poor condition since at least 2009 — something that can easily be verified through Google Maps imagery — although it’s very likely it has been this way for even longer. Sixteen years is quite unacceptable. Last year, after numerous complaints on this platform (more than ten), a small section of the street was finally repaired. However, both Elmfield Avenue and Elmfield Street remain in a severe state of neglect. There are potholes throughout, growing larger each time, and in this year’s 2025 photo — next to the maroon car — you can clearly see that the ground is sinking, creating a large puddle every time it rains. The pavements are also in very poor condition: cracked, uneven, and full of holes. I intend to continue reporting these issues regularly until appropriate maintenance work is carried out. Sixteen years without proper repairs is more than enough. Would be nice for somene to do something for once

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 11a, Elmfield Road, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: More than one relatively small potholes bad enough for drivers to take avoiding action, 9th March
item.description:
Junction of Swindon Road and Windyridge Road. Potholes at this junction have been a persistent problem and while the worst have been addressed, lesser ones have been ignored.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 394, Swindon Road, Kingsditch, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
